title: Term of the Week: Net Interest Income
text: Higher interest rates boosted big banks' net interest income in the second quarter, which is the profit banks earn from charging higher interest rates to borrowers than they pay out to depositors and account holders.

title: A Hidden Gem: The Bank Stock Flying Under The Radar
text: We've already kicked off our coverage of the financial sector's earnings season, with some of the titans like Goldman Sachs Group Inc NYSE: GS among the heaviest traded stocks this week. But there's one bank stock, whose roots go back even further than Goldman, that we think has been flying under the radar.

title: 7 Upcoming Dividend Increases, Including A Dividend King
text: Companies with a history of increasing dividends usually perform better and signify robust business performance and shareholder reward commitment. The article provides a list of companies expected to increase their dividends in the coming week, compiled from the "U.S. Dividend Champions" spreadsheet and NASDAQ data. The article also explains the ex-dividend date, dividend streak categories, and provides additional metrics and historical returns for these companies.

title: BNY Mellon Joins the FedNow® Service, Advancing U.S. Payment System
text: NEW YORK , July 20, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- BNY Mellon (NYSE: BK) today announced it is now live on the Federal Reserve's new instant payment rail, the FedNow Service, making it one of the first banks to begin sending and receiving payments from participating institutions and support payment activity through settlement or liquidity services. FedNow will operate 24 hours a day, seven days a week, year-round.

title: The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation (BK) Q2 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation (NYSE:BK ) Q2 2023 Earnings Conference Call July 18, 2023 10:30 AM ET Company Participants Marius Merz - Head of Investor Relations Robin Vince - President and Chief Executive Officer Dermot McDonogh - Chief Financial Officer Conference Call Participants Brennan Hawken - UBS Alex Blostein - Goldman Sachs Betsy Graseck - Morgan Stanley Steven Chubak - Wolfe Research Glenn Schorr - Evercore ISI Ebrahim Poonawala - Bank of America Securities Kenneth Usdin - Jefferies Brian Bedell - Deutsche Bank Gerard Cassidy - RBC Capital Markets Rob Wildhack - Autonomous Research Rajiv Bhatia - Morningstar Operator Good morning, and welcome to the 2023 Second Quarter Earnings Conference Call hosted by BNY Mellon. At this time, all participants are in a listen-only mode.

title: BNY Mellon (BK) Beats on Q2 Earnings as Revenues Increase Y/Y
text: BNY Mellon (BK) records a rise in revenues and marginally lower expenses in the second quarter of 2023.

title: The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation (BK) Q2 Earnings: Taking a Look at Key Metrics Versus Estimates
text: Although the revenue and EPS for The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation (BK) give a sense of how its business performed in the quarter ended June 2023, it might be worth considering how some key metrics compare with Wall Street estimates and the year-ago numbers.

title: BNY Mellon beats 2Q estimates as Securities Services unit soars
text: BNY Mellon (NYSE:BK) has reported a strong rise in second-quarter earnings, supported by its Securities Services and Market and Wealth Services businesses. The financial services posted a 5% rise in total revenue to $4.5 billion for the three months to June 30, 2023, as a 33% increase in net interest revenue made up for a 2% decline in fee revenue.

title: BNY Mellon Declares Dividends
text: NEW YORK , July 18, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation (NYSE: BK) today announced that its Board of Directors declared dividends on its common and preferred stock as follows: Common – a quarterly common stock dividend of $0.42 per share, payable on August 10, 2023 to shareholders of record as of the close of business on July 28, 2023. Preferred – the following dividends for the noncumulative perpetual preferred stock, liquidation preference $100,000 per share, for the dividend period ending in September 2023, in each case payable on September 20, 2023 to holders of record as of the close of business on September 5, 2023: $1,552.50 per share on the Series A Preferred Stock (equivalent to $15.525000 per Normal Preferred Capital Security of Mellon Capital IV, each representing a 1/100th interest in a share of the Series A Preferred Stock); $2,036.78 per share on the Series D Preferred Stock (equivalent to $20.367778 per depositary share, each representing a 1/100th interest in a share of the Series D Preferred Stock); $2,312.50 per share on the Series F Preferred Stock (equivalent to $23.125000 per depositary share, each representing a 1/100th interest in a share of the Series F Preferred Stock); $2,350.00 per share on the Series G Preferred Stock (equivalent to $23.500000 per depositary share, each representing a 1/100th interest in a share of the Series G Preferred Stock); $925.00 per share on the Series H Preferred Stock (equivalent to $9.250000 per depositary share, each representing a 1/100th interest in a share of the Series H Preferred Stock); and $937.50 per share on the Series I Preferred Stock (equivalent to $9.375000 per depositary share, each representing a 1/100th interest in a share of the Series I Preferred Stock).

title: BNY Mellon Reports Second Quarter 2023 Results
text: NEW YORK , July 18, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation (NYSE: BK) has reported financial results for the second quarter 2023. The company's earnings release along with the quarterly financial highlights and other earnings-related documents are available at www.bnymellon.com/investorrelations.

title: BNY Mellon beats second-quarter profit estimates on higher rates
text: Bank of New York Mellon Corp beat Wall Street targets for second-quarter profit on Tuesday as the U.S. Federal Reserve's aggressive policy tightening boosted income earned from interest payments on loans.
